content_en:
“And verily, we are able to take it away “When these things are raised from the earth, its inhabitants will be forbidden the good of the world and of the Hereafter.” (Related by Al-Haafidh Ad-Diyaa) This hadith is very much Ghareeb; rather, it is Munkar. Salamah Ibn ‘Alee, one of the narrators, is considered to be weak by the Imams.

‘Allah 3 described the rivers of Paradise with the abundance of their flowing. And the people of Paradise will direct their flowing as they please, meaning that they will draw out water from it from any place they wish. Different kinds of courses and waters will be sent to them. Ibn Mas’ood # said, “There is no spring in Paradise except that it gushes forth from underneath a mountain of musk (best kind of perfume).” Al’Amash related that Ibn Mas’ood - said, “The rivers of Paradise gush forth from a mountain of musk (the best

650 Book of The End: Great Trials and Tribulations

The Trees Of Paradise Allah 48 said:

bas Tat tot G al

tal Ga Gh GN

“But those who believe (in the Oneness of Allah %-Islamic Monotheism) and do deeds of righteousness, We shall admit them to Garden under which rivers flow (Paradise), abiding therein forever. Therein they shall have Azwajun Mutalthharatun (purified mates or wives), and We shall admit them to shades wide and ever deepening (Paradise)

And Allah 4 said:

Book of The End : Great Trials and Tribulations 651

“With spreading branches. Then which of the blessings of your Lord will you both (jinn and men deny)?’ And Allah 4% said:

“Dark green (in color)’

This means that their greenness is so severe, they are leaning towards blackness; it also refers to the intertwining of their trees.

And Allah # said: 406 BEN SS Hey GE GB & ose

“Reclining upon the couches lined with silk brocade, and the fruits of the two Gardens will be near at hand?)

And Allah 86 said: "The fruits in branches whereof will be low and near at hand’) And Allah 3% said:

45 GM SB Ub 4S

pot lS 0 pe J 3.0 gal OAT G sacll CU} ELEY 0 gS BSS 0 LK UG 0 wk Bo

whe ote ish go O dese YD

“And those on the Right Hand — how (fortunate) will be those on

652 Book of The End : Great Trials and Tribulations

the Right Hand? (They will be) among thornless lote-trees, And among Talh (banana trees) with fruits piled one above another, And in the shade long-extended. And by water flowing constantly, And fruit in plenty, whose supply is not cut off (by change of season), nor are they out of reach, And on couches or thrones, raised high)

And Allah # said:

“In them (both) will be fruits, and date palins and pomegranates?

And Allah 3# said: see eee Ube can 40S KS F oe tad “In them (both) will be every kind of fruit in pairs’ Abu Elurairah # reported that the Messenger of Allah 2 said, “There is no tree in Paradise except that its trunk is of gold.” (Related by Abu

Bakr ibn Abee Daawood) At-Tirmidhee related it through another chain and said, “Hasan sahih.”

Ibn ‘Abbaas .& said, “The roots of date palms in Paradise are of green emeralds. And their branches are red gold. And their leaves are clothing for the people of Paradise... Its fruits... are whiter than milk and purer than honey...” (Related by Abu Bakr ibn Abee AdDunyaa)

In another narration, Ibn ‘Abbaas i said, ““Az-Zil Al-Mamdood (The Shade Long-Extendedy is a tree in Paradise on a trunk. As for its distance, the diligent rider travels under its shade for 100 years,” meaning all of its sides. He said, “The people of Paradise, the people of Al-Ghuraf (the rooms), and others will come out and talk underneath its shade.” He said, “Then some of them will desire, remembering the amusement of the world, and then Allah will send a wind from Paradise, and He will shake that tree with every amusement that was in the world.” (Related by Ibn Abee Ad-

Book of The End: Great Trials and Tribulations 653

There Is A Tree In Paradise, Under The Shade Of Which, A Racing, Mudammir (Well Equipped) Rider Travels For 100 Years Without Getting Across It

Sahl ibn Sa’ad # reported that the Messenger of Allah #8 said, “Verily, in Paradise there is a ree, under the shade of which the rider travels for 100 years without getting across it.” (Bukhaaree and Muslim)

In another narration, Abu Sa’eed Al-Khudree # reported that the Prophet # said, “Verily, in Paradise there is such a tree that a racing, well-equipped, fast rider travels under its shade for 100 years without getting across it.” (Bukhaaree)

And as to the saying of Allah #8: qs jp “And in shade long-extended” “

Anas # relaied that the Prophet # said, “In Paradise there is a tree, underneath the shade of which the rider travels for 100 years.” (Bukhaaree)

In another narration, Abu Hurairah reported that the Prophet # said, “In Paradise there is a tree, underneath the shade of which the rider travels for 100 years. Recite if you wish: And in shade longextended (Quran 56:30).” (Ahmad)

underneath the shade of which the rider travels for 100 years without getting across it.”

Abu Hurairah reported that the Messenger of Allah 2% said, “In Paradise there ig a tree, underneath the shade of which the rider travels for 100 years.”

654 Book of The End: Great Trials and Tribulations

Abu Hurairah reported that he heard the Messenger of Allah 2% say, “In Paradise there is a tree, underneath the shade of which the rider travels for 100 years.” (Ahmad)

And in another narration Abu Hurairah 4 related that he heard Abul-Qaasim (Muhammad #&) say, “In Paradise there is a tree, underneath the shade of which the rider travels for 100 years without getting across it.” (Ahmad)

Abu Hurairah .& reported that the Prophet # said, “Verily, in Paradise there is a tree, underneath the shade of which the rider travels for 70 - or 100. It is the AL_Khuld (Eternal) Tree.” (Ahmad)

Abu Sa’eed & reported that a man said, “O Messenger of Allah, Tooba (literally, blessed is he) for he who sees you and believes in you.” The Prophet # said, “Tooba for he who sees me and believes in me, and then Tooba for he who believes in me but hasn’t seen

Book of The End: Great Trials and Tribulations 655

me.” A man said, “O Messenger of Allah, and what is Tooba?” Ele 2 said, “A tree in Paradise, (the size of which is) the traveling distance of 100 years. The garments of the people of Paradise come out from their sprouts.” (Related by Hurmulah Ibn ‘Abdullah Ibn Wahb)

Sidratul-Muntaha Allah 4¢ said: 0 GE ke 0 BAT OL ke 0 GHG tt >

Bh Bo 6 BS to BEG HT

“And indeed he (Muhammad #8) saw him (Jibreel) at @ second

descent (i.e. another time). Near Sidartul-Muntaha (lote-tree of the

utmost boundary, beyond which none can pass). Near it is the

Paradise of Abode. When that covered the lote-tree which did cover

it] The sight (of Prophet Muhammad #8) turned not aside (right or

left), nor it transgressed beyond (the) limit (ordained for it). Indeed

he (Muhammad 38) did see, of the Greatest Signs, of his Lord

(Allahy’ As for the verse, “When that covered the lote-tree which did cover it,” we mentioned that the light of the Lord Jala Jalaatuhu (Glorified is He in His Greatness and Majesty) covered it; and the angels covered it. Upon it is Ghurbaan, meaning abundance. And moths of gold covered it, and many colors covered it. The Messenger of Allah 2% said, “Different colors cover it; I do not know what they are, and no one can describe them.” The following is mentioned in the hadith of Al-Mai’raaj (the Prophet's ascension to the heavens): “Then I was raised to SidratulMuntaha, in the seventh heaven. Sts Nabiq (its fruit, which is similar to a grape before it becomes very red) is like Qilaal (large jars) Hajar. ‘And its leaves are like the ears of elephants. Two external rivers come out of its trunk, and two internal rivers. I said, ‘O Jibreel, what is this?’ He said, ‘As for the internal rivers, they are in Paradise. And as for the external rivers, they are the Nile and the Euphrates.” (Bukhaaree and Muslim)
content_id:
"Dan sesungguhnya, Kami mampu mengambilnya." "Ketika hal-hal ini diangkat dari bumi, penduduknya akan dilarang dari kebaikan dunia dan akhirat." (Diriwayatkan oleh Al-Haafidzh Ad-Dhiyaa) Hadis ini sangat Gharib; bahkan, ia Munkar. Salamah ibn 'Alee, salah satu perawinya, dianggap lemah oleh para Imam.

Allah عز وجل menggambarkan sungai-sungai Surga dengan kelimpahan alirannya. Dan penghuni Surga akan mengarahkan alirannya sesuka mereka, artinya mereka akan mengeluarkan air darinya dari tempat mana pun yang mereka inginkan. Berbagai jenis aliran dan air akan dikirimkan kepada mereka. Ibnu Mas'ood رضي الله عنه berkata, "Tidak ada mata air di Surga kecuali ia mengalir dari bawah sebuah gunung kesturi (jenis parfum terbaik)." Al-'Amasy meriwayatkan bahwa Ibnu Mas'ood رضي الله عنه berkata, "Sungai-sungai Surga mengalir dari sebuah gunung kesturi (jenis

650 Kitab Akhir Zaman: Fitnah dan Cobaan Besar

Pohon-Pohon Surga. Allah عز وجل berfirman:

"Tetapi mereka yang beriman (dalam Keesaan Allah عز وجل — Tauhid Islam) dan mengerjakan amal saleh, Kami akan memasukkan mereka ke dalam taman-taman yang di bawahnya mengalir sungai-sungai (Surga), tinggal di dalamnya selamanya. Di dalamnya mereka akan mendapatkan Azwajun Muthahhara (pasangan atau istri-istri yang suci), dan Kami akan memasukkan mereka ke dalam naungan yang luas dan semakin dalam (Surga)."

Dan Allah عز وجل berfirman:

Kitab Akhir Zaman: Fitnah dan Cobaan Besar 651

"Dengan ranting-ranting yang mengembang. Maka nikmat Tuhan kalian yang manakah yang kalian berdua (jin dan manusia) dustakan?" Dan Allah عز وجل berfirman:

"Hijau tua (warnanya)."

Ini berarti bahwa kehijauannya sangat padat sehingga condong ke warna hitam; ini juga mengacu pada saling berjalitnya pohon-pohon mereka.

Dan Allah عز وجل berfirman:

"Berbaring di atas dipan-dipan yang dilapisi sutra brokat, dan buah-buahan dari kedua Taman mudah dijangkau."

Dan Allah عز وجل berfirman: "Buah-buahan dalam dahan-dahannya yang akan rendah dan mudah dijangkau." Dan Allah عز وجل berfirman:

"Dan mereka yang berada di sebelah kanan — betapa (beruntung) mereka yang ada di sebelah kanan?

652 Kitab Akhir Zaman: Fitnah dan Cobaan Besar

(Mereka akan berada) di antara pohon-pohon bidara yang tidak berduri, Dan di antara pohon Talh (pohon pisang) dengan buah-buahan yang bertumpuk satu di atas yang lain, Dan di bawah naungan yang sangat panjang. Dan di dekat air yang terus mengalir, Dan buah-buahan yang berlimpah, yang persediaannya tidak terputus (karena pergantian musim), dan tidak pula tidak terjangkau, Dan di atas dipan-dipan atau singgasana yang ditinggikan."

Dan Allah عز وجل berfirman:

"Di dalamnya (keduanya) ada buah-buahan, pohon kurma, dan delima."

Dan Allah عز وجل berfirman: "Di dalamnya (keduanya) ada setiap jenis buah-buahan berpasangan." Abu Hurairah رضي الله عنه melaporkan bahwa Rasulullah صلى الله عليه وسلم bersabda, "Tidak ada pohon di Surga kecuali batangnya terbuat dari emas." (Diriwayatkan oleh Abu

Bakr ibn Abee Daawood) At-Tirmidzi meriwayatkannya melalui rantai lain dan berkata, "Hasan sahih."

Ibnu 'Abbaas رضي الله عنه berkata, "Akar-akar pohon kurma di Surga terbuat dari zamrud hijau. Dan cabang-cabangnya adalah emas merah. Dan daun-daunnya adalah pakaian bagi penghuni Surga... Buah-buahnya... lebih putih dari susu dan lebih murni dari madu..." (Diriwayatkan oleh Abu Bakr ibn Abee Ad-Dunyaa)

Dalam riwayat lain, Ibnu 'Abbaas رضي الله عنه berkata, "Az-Zhillu Al-Mamdood (Naungan yang Sangat Panjang) adalah sebuah pohon di Surga di atas sebuah batang. Adapun jaraknya, penunggang kuda yang rajin melakukan perjalanan di bawah naungannya selama 100 tahun," artinya semua sisinya. Dia berkata, "Penghuni Surga, penghuni Al-Ghuraf (kamar-kamar), dan lainnya akan keluar dan berbicara di bawah naungannya." Dia berkata, "Kemudian sebagian dari mereka akan berhasrat, mengingat hiburan dunia, kemudian Allah akan mengirimkan angin dari Surga, dan Dia akan menggoyangkan pohon itu dengan setiap hiburan yang ada di dunia." (Diriwayatkan oleh Ibnu Abee Ad-

Kitab Akhir Zaman: Fitnah dan Cobaan Besar 653

Ada Sebuah Pohon di Surga, yang di Bawah Naungannya, Seorang Penunggang Kuda yang Berlari Kencang dan Lengkap Perlengkapannya Melakukan Perjalanan Selama 100 Tahun Tanpa Bisa Menyeberanginya

Sahl ibn Sa'ad رضي الله عنه melaporkan bahwa Rasulullah صلى الله عليه وسلم bersabda, "Sesungguhnya, di Surga ada sebuah pohon, yang di bawah naungannya seorang penunggang kuda melakukan perjalanan selama 100 tahun tanpa bisa menyeberanginya." (Bukhari dan Muslim)

Dalam riwayat lain, Abu Sa'eed Al-Khudree رضي الله عنه melaporkan bahwa Nabi صلى الله عليه وسلم bersabda, "Sesungguhnya, di Surga ada pohon sedemikian rupa sehingga seorang penunggang kuda yang berlari kencang, lengkap perlengkapannya, dan cepat melakukan perjalanan di bawah naungannya selama 100 tahun tanpa bisa menyeberanginya." (Bukhari)

Dan mengenai firman Allah عز وجل: "Dan di bawah naungan yang sangat panjang"

Anas رضي الله عنه meriwayatkan bahwa Nabi صلى الله عليه وسلم bersabda, "Di Surga ada sebuah pohon, yang di bawah naungannya seorang penunggang kuda melakukan perjalanan selama 100 tahun." (Bukhari)

Dalam riwayat lain, Abu Hurairah melaporkan bahwa Nabi صلى الله عليه وسلم bersabda, "Di Surga ada sebuah pohon, yang di bawah naungannya seorang penunggang kuda melakukan perjalanan selama 100 tahun. Bacalah jika kamu mau: Dan di bawah naungan yang sangat panjang (Quran 56:30)." (Ahmad)

di bawah naungannya seorang penunggang kuda melakukan perjalanan selama 100 tahun tanpa bisa menyeberanginya."

Abu Hurairah melaporkan bahwa Rasulullah صلى الله عليه وسلم bersabda, "Di Surga ada sebuah pohon, yang di bawah naungannya seorang penunggang kuda melakukan perjalanan selama 100 tahun."

654 Kitab Akhir Zaman: Fitnah dan Cobaan Besar

Abu Hurairah melaporkan bahwa dia mendengar Rasulullah صلى الله عليه وسلم bersabda, "Di Surga ada sebuah pohon, yang di bawah naungannya seorang penunggang kuda melakukan perjalanan selama 100 tahun." (Ahmad)

Dan dalam riwayat lain Abu Hurairah رضي الله عنه meriwayatkan bahwa dia mendengar Abul-Qaasim (Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم) bersabda, "Di Surga ada sebuah pohon, yang di bawah naungannya seorang penunggang kuda melakukan perjalanan selama 100 tahun tanpa bisa menyeberanginya." (Ahmad)

Abu Hurairah رضي الله عنه melaporkan bahwa Nabi صلى الله عليه وسلم bersabda, "Sesungguhnya, di Surga ada sebuah pohon, yang di bawah naungannya seorang penunggang kuda melakukan perjalanan selama 70 — atau 100. Itu adalah Pohon Al-Khuld (Abadi)." (Ahmad)

Abu Sa'eed رضي الله عنه melaporkan bahwa seorang pria berkata, "Wahai Rasulullah, Tuuba (secara harfiah, berbahagialah dia) bagi orang yang melihatmu dan beriman kepadamu." Nabi صلى الله عليه وسلم bersabda, "Tuuba bagi orang yang melihatku dan beriman kepadaku, kemudian Tuuba bagi orang yang beriman kepadaku tetapi belum melihatku.

Kitab Akhir Zaman: Fitnah dan Cobaan Besar 655

aku." Seorang pria berkata, "Wahai Rasulullah, dan apakah Tuuba itu?" Beliau صلى الله عليه وسلم bersabda, "Sebuah pohon di Surga, (ukurannya adalah) jarak perjalanan 100 tahun. Pakaian-pakaian penghuni Surga keluar dari tunas-tunasnya." (Diriwayatkan oleh Hurmalah ibn 'Abdullah ibn Wahb)

Sidratul-Muntahaa. Allah عز وجل berfirman:

"Dan sesungguhnya dia (Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم) melihatnya (Jibril) pada turun yang kedua (yaitu waktu lain). Di dekat Sidratul-Muntahaa (pohon bidara yang berada di batas paling akhir, yang tidak ada yang bisa melewatinya). Di dekatnya adalah Surga Tempat Tinggal. Ketika sesuatu menutupi pohon bidara itu yang menutupinya. Pandangan (Nabi Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم) tidak menyimpang (kanan atau kiri), dan tidak pula melampaui batas (yang ditetapkan untuknya). Sesungguhnya dia (Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم) benar-benar melihat, dari Tanda-Tanda Terbesar, Tuhannya (Allah)." Adapun ayat, "Ketika sesuatu menutupi pohon bidara itu yang menutupinya," kami menyebutkan bahwa cahaya Tuhan Jalla Jalaatuhu (Dimuliakan Dia dalam Kebesaran dan Keagungan-Nya) menutupinya; dan para malaikat menutupinya. Di atasnya ada Ghurbaan, artinya kelimpahan. Dan ngengat-ngengat emas menutupinya, dan banyak warna menutupinya. Rasulullah صلى الله عليه وسلم bersabda, "Berbagai warna menutupinya; aku tidak tahu apa mereka, dan tidak ada yang bisa menggambarkan mereka." Berikut ini disebutkan dalam hadis Al-Mi'raaj (kenaikan Nabi صلى الله عليه وسلم ke langit): "Kemudian aku diangkat ke Sidratul-Muntahaa, di langit ketujuh. Nabiq-nya (buahnya, yang mirip dengan anggur sebelum menjadi sangat merah) seperti Qilaal (toples besar) Hajar. Dan daun-daunnya seperti telinga gajah. Dua sungai eksternal keluar dari batangnya, dan dua sungai internal. Aku berkata, 'Wahai Jibril, apakah ini?' Dia berkata, 'Adapun sungai-sungai internal, mereka ada di Surga. Dan adapun sungai-sungai eksternal, itu adalah Sungai Nil dan Eufrat.'" (Bukhari dan Muslim)

Salamah Ibn ‘Alee, one of the narrators, is considered to be weak by the Imams. ‘الله 3 described the rivers of الجنة with the abundance of their flowing. And the people of الجنة will direct their flowing as they please, meaning that they will draw out water from it from any place they wish. Different kinds of courses and waters will be sent to them. Near Sidartul-Muntaha (lote-tree of the utmost boundary, beyond which none can pass). Near it is the الجنة of Abode. عندما that covered the lote-tree which did cover it] The sight (of النبي محمد ﷺ #8) turned not aside (الحق or left), nor it transgressed beyond (the) limit (ordained for it). Indeed he (محمد ﷺ 38) did see, of the Greatest Signs, of his Lord (Allahy’ أما the الآية, “عندما that covered the lote-tree which did cover it,” we mentioned that the light of the Lord Jala Jalaatuhu (Glorified is He in His Greatness and Majesty) covered it; and the الملائكة covered it. Upon it is Ghurbaan, meaning abundance. And moths of gold covered it, and many colors covered it. الرسول ﷺ of الله 2% said, “Different colors cover it; I do not know what they are, and no one can describe them.” The following is mentioned in the الحديث of Al-Mai’raaj (النبي ﷺ's ascension to the heavens): “Then I was raised to SidratulMuntaha, in the سابعاً heaven. Sts Nabiq (its fruit, which is similar to a grape قبل it becomes very red) is like Qilaal (large jars) Hajar. ‘And its leaves are like the ears of elephants. Two external rivers come out of its trunk, and two internal rivers. I said, ‘O Jibreel, what is this?’ قال, ‘أما the internal rivers, they are in الجنة. And أما the external rivers, they are the Nile and the Euphrates.” (Bukhaaree and مسلم)
